summary_strategy: Reframe summary to highlight offensive security aptitude through the lens of hardening and AI red teaming—emphasizing deployable controls, operational reliability, and mission-driven execution aligned with wraithwatch’s SpaceX/Palantir/Anduril ethos
skills_strategy: Use condensed 3-line format to prioritize Security Engineering & Automation, Cloud/Platform Integrations, and AI-driven SecOps—embedding keywords like 'penetration testing context', 'EDR bypass awareness', and 'agentic AI for red teaming' without overclaiming

experience_focus.must_highlight: vulnerability remediation automation, endpoint hardening workflows, AI/red teaming adjacent capabilities
experience_focus.safe_metrics_to_keep: 10,000+ devices, 25 percent compliance improvement, 200 hours saved annually
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: no direct penetration testing experience claimed, no false claims about C2 frameworks or exploit development

risk_controls: Never state 'penetration testing' unless explicitly documented—replace with 'offensive security context', 'attack simulation', or 'vulnerability exploitation modeling'
Avoid phrases like 'hacker mindset'—use 'adversarial thinking', 'threat-informed hardening', or 'exploit-aware remediation'
Do not invent tools, frameworks, or methodologies beyond those listed in source material (e.g., no Cobalt Strike, Metasploit, or custom shellcode unless stated)
Replace all speculative metrics with qualitative claims (e.g., 'accelerated patch deployment' instead of unverified time-to-remediate numbers)
